# Template: Research Output Report
# Generated by: customer-researcher agent
# Consumed by: solution-architect agent, pitch-designer agent
# Format: Structured markdown — all sections required; mark "NOT FOUND" if unavailable

---

## Research Report

**Company:** {company_name}
**Research date:** {date}
**Analyst:** customer-researcher agent
**Confidence level:** HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW
**Sources used:** {n} independent sources

---

## 1. Executive Summary

> 3–5 sentences capturing the most important findings for a seller preparing for a first meeting.
> What is the company? Where are they in their journey? What's the most important signal?

{Executive summary text}

**Top 3 signals for seller:**
1. {Signal 1 — e.g., "Board-level mandate for AI — CEO mentioned it in 3 public statements this quarter"}
2. {Signal 2 — e.g., "AWS contract likely renewing in Q3 — window of opportunity"}
3. {Signal 3 — e.g., "Hiring 12 data engineers — confirms data platform investment intent"}

## 2. Company Profile

```yaml
company:
  legal_name: "{Full legal name}"
  brand_name: "{Common name}"
  legal_form: "{GmbH / SA / sp. z o.o. / PLC / etc.}"
  founded: {year}
  headquarters: "{City, Country}"
  other_offices: ["{City, Country}", "{City, Country}"]
  website: "https://{domain}"

financials:
  revenue_annual: "{€/$/PLN X million — year}"
  revenue_growth_yoy: "{X% — year}"
  ebitda_margin: "{X% or NOT CONFIRMED}"
  funding_stage: "{Public / Private / PE-backed / VC-backed / bootstrapped}"
  last_funding_round: "{Series X / €Xm / year — or NOT APPLICABLE}"
  stock_exchange: "{Exchange:TICKER — or NOT LISTED}"
  source_confidence: "{CONFIRMED from filings / ESTIMATED from industry data / NOT FOUND}"

scale:
  employees_total: {number or range}
  employees_it: {number or ESTIMATED}
  employees_tech_dev: {number or ESTIMATED}
  customers: "{B2B / B2C / both — size/type}"
  countries_operating: {number}
  transaction_volume: "{X per day/month — or NOT FOUND}"

industry:
  primary: "{e.g., Financial Services}"
  sub_sector: "{e.g., Insurance — Commercial P&C}"
  nace_code: "{e.g., K65.12}"
  regulatory_environment: ["{GDPR}", "{NIS2}", "{Solvency II}", "{etc.}"]
```

## 6. Pain Point Map

> Inferred pain points mapped to evidence. Confidence indicates strength of evidence.

| Pain Point | Evidence | Confidence | Relevant GCP Solution |
|------------|---------|------------|----------------------|
| {Pain 1 — e.g., "High infrastructure cost — hardware refresh due"} | {Job posting / news / interview quote} | HIGH | {e.g., GCE + CUDs + migration services} |
| {Pain 2 — e.g., "Data silos — analytics team can't access operational data"} | {Evidence} | MEDIUM | {e.g., BigQuery + Datastream} |
| {Pain 3 — e.g., "Security posture gap — CISO hire signal"} | {Evidence} | MEDIUM | {e.g., SCC + BeyondCorp} |
| {Pain 4 — e.g., "Slow deployment cycles — multiple DevOps job openings"} | {Evidence} | LOW (inferred) | {e.g., GKE + Cloud Build} |
| {Pain 5 — e.g., "Collaboration inefficiency — Microsoft 365 complaints on Glassdoor"} | {Evidence} | LOW (inferred) | {e.g., Google Workspace} |

## 10. Risk Factors

| Risk | Type | Severity | Mitigation |
|------|------|---------|-----------|
| {Risk 1 — e.g., "3-year AWS commit recently signed"} | Commercial | High | Target new workloads outside commit scope |
| {Risk 2 — e.g., "CISO known to be skeptical of Google"} | Relationship | Medium | Engage through technical track; build CISO credibility first |
| {Risk 3 — e.g., "Low confidence data — small public footprint"} | Data quality | Medium | Use industry defaults; validate in first meeting |
